assertions.title: Authenticated requests receive a Bedrock or Z.AI response through the gateway

steps.expected: Auth entry page loads with Register/Sign In options
steps.observed: Auth entry page loaded successfully showing Register, Sign In, and Register with Passkey buttons

steps.action: Set up WebAuthn virtual authenticator via CDP
steps.expected: Virtual authenticator created and available for passkey registration
steps.observed: Virtual authenticator created with ID e9cdab83-3f8d-40f5-9b86-ce748078523b on CDP port 9223

steps.action: Enter username 'gatewaytest74d5df63' and click Register with Passkey
steps.expected: WebAuthn registration ceremony completes and session is established
steps.observed: Registration completed successfully. User authenticated and shell UI displayed with Sign Out button

steps.expected: Bootstrap returns sandbox assignment and user context

steps.observed: Live Channel shows 'Connected' - WebSocket through proxy is working

steps.expected: Task accepted and routed through gateway to provider
steps.observed: Endpoint returned HTTP 404 with error 'not found'. Runtime API not available on deployed origin

steps.action: Verify response path through gateway (proxy → runtime/VM → gateway → provider → UI)
steps.expected: Response returns through proper path without direct browser-to-provider calls
steps.observed: Could not verify - runtime task endpoint unavailable. Network trace shows only draft.choir-ip.com traffic (no direct provider calls attempted)

assertions.issues: Runtime task API endpoints (/api/agent/task, /api/events) return 404 on deployed origin. Shell bootstrap works (sandbox-m1 assigned) but runtime/gateway flow cannot be tested due to missing backend. This appears to be a deployment/infrastructure issue where the runtime/sandbox services are not fully deployed or the VM routing is not configured on Node B.

frictions.description: WebAuthn virtual authenticator setup required CDP connection - standard agent-browser session doesn't support WebAuthn without external Chromium with CDP
frictions.resolution: Launched separate Chromium with --remote-debugging-port=9223, set up virtual authenticator via CDP, then connected agent-browser to that port

frictions.description: Runtime task endpoints (/api/agent/task) return 404 instead of accepting task submissions
frictions.resolution: Identified as deployment issue - runtime/sandbox not fully deployed on Node B origin

blockers.description: Runtime API endpoints (/api/agent/task, /api/agent/status, /api/events) return 404 on deployed origin https://draft.choir-ip.com. Shell bootstrap works (returns sandbox-m1 assignment), but task submission endpoints are not available. This prevents testing the full gateway flow: proxy → runtime/VM → gateway → Bedrock/Z.AI → UI response.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Verified auth is working (bootstrap returns user context), verified proxy routing exists in code (HandleProtectedAPI), checked service health endpoints (all 404). Issue appears to be that runtime/sandbox services are not deployed or configured on Node B.

summary: Partially tested VAL-GATEWAY-001. Successfully demonstrated: (1) WebAuthn passkey authentication working through deployed origin, (2) Shell bootstrap returning sandbox assignment through proxy, (3) Live WebSocket channel connecting through proxy, (4) Network traffic limited to draft.choir-ip.com (no direct provider calls). However, the test is blocked because runtime task API endpoints (/api/agent/task) return 404, preventing verification of the full gateway flow to upstream providers. The proxy auth gating and routing logic works, but the backend runtime/gateway services appear not fully deployed on Node B.
